content.p: Use the Integral Test to determine if \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty {{summand}} converges.

outtro.p:
Note that \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle {{integrand}} is a positive, eventually decreasing function. We have that:
\renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \int_{ {{k0}} }^\infty {{integrand}} dx= \lim_{c\to \infty}\int_{ {{k0}} }^c {{integrand}} dx = \lim_{c\to \infty} \left( {{integral}} \right) \Big|_{ {{k0}} }^c \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} = \lim_{c\to \infty} \left( \left( {{up}} \right) - \left({{down}}\right) \right) = {{limit}}.
The integral \displaystyle \int_{ {{k0}} }^\infty {{integrand}} dx converges and thus by the Integral Test, \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty {{summand}} converges as well.

content.p: Use the Integral Test to determine if \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty {{summand}} converges.

outtro.p:
Note that \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle {{integrand}} is a positive, eventually decreasing function. We have that:
\renewcommand{\log}{\ln} \displaystyle \int_{ {{k0}} }^\infty {{integrand}} dx= \lim_{c\to \infty}\int_{ {{k0}} }^c {{integrand}} dx = \lim_{c\to \infty} \left( {{integral}} \right) \Big|_{ {{k0}} }^c \renewcommand{\log}{\ln} = \lim_{c\to \infty} \left( \left( {{up}} \right) - \left({{down}}\right) \right) = \infty.
The integral \displaystyle \int_{ {{k0}} }^\infty {{integrand}} dx diverges and thus by the Integral Test, \displaystyle \sum_{ n= {{k0}} }^\infty {{summand}} diverges as well.
